---
title: "Quarto Feature Demo"
subtitle: "Testing many Quarto capabilities"
author: "Yasiru Karunanayaka"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: false
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  error: false
---

# Introduction

This page demonstrates many **Quarto features**.

Inline math example: $E = mc^2$

Displayed equation:

$$
\frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial t^2} = c^2 \nabla^2 u
$$

## Lists

### Unordered

- Physics
- Mathematics
- Simulations
- Geometry

### Ordered

1. Write page
2. Preview
3. Edit
4. Publish

---

# Callouts

::: {.callout-note}
## Note
Quarto pages are written mostly in Markdown.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Tip
Great for **scientific notes and research logs**.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
## Warning
Large pages should be split into multiple `.qmd` files.
:::

---

# Table Example

| Quantity | Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| Frequency | $f$ | cycles per second |
| Angular frequency | $\omega$ | $2\pi f$ |
| Wavelength | $\lambda$ | spatial period |

---

# Complex Equation

$$
\beta = k_0\sqrt{(1+X_s^2-R_s^2-2jR_sX_s)}
$$

Aligned equations:

$$
\begin{aligned}
z(t) &= E(t)e^{i\Omega t} \\
E(t) &= E_0 e^{\phi t}
\end{aligned}
$$

---

# Code Block Example

```python
import numpy as np

x = np.linspace(0,10,5)
y = x**2

print(y)
```

---

# Python Plot Example

```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(0,10,200)
y = np.sin(x)*np.exp(-0.1*x)

plt.plot(x,y)
plt.title("Damped Oscillation")
plt.xlabel("x")
plt.ylabel("y")
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()
```

---

# Columns Layout

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

## Left Side

Some notes:

- wave propagation
- damping
- phase

$$
x(t) = A\cos(\omega t)
$$

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

## Right Side

More text here.

You can place equations or figures.

$$
y(t) = A\sin(\omega t)
$$

:::

:::

---

# HTML Example

<div style="padding:1rem;border:1px solid gray;border-radius:8px">
This is a custom HTML box inside Quarto.
</div>

---

# Desmos Embed

<iframe
 src="https://www.desmos.com/calculator/abcd1234?embed"
 width="700"
 height="400"
 style="border:1px solid #ccc"
 frameborder=0>
</iframe>

Replace `abcd1234` with your own Desmos graph ID.

---

# Footnote Example

This sentence has a footnote.[^1]

[^1]: Quarto supports Markdown footnotes.

---

# Quote

> Mathematics is the language in which God has written the universe.

---

# Conclusion

This page demonstrated:

- Markdown
- equations
- tables
- callouts
- Python execution
- plots
- HTML
- Desmos embedding
- layout columns

You can use Quarto as a **scientific notebook or website system**.
